name: 2021 Iranian Presidential Election
description: The June 2021 presidential election produced one of the lowest turnouts in the Islamic Republic's history — approximately 48% nationally, and around 26% in Tehran. The [Guardian Council](/orgs/guardian-council) disqualified 585 of 592 candidates who registered, approving only seven to run. The disqualifications included former Majlis Speaker [Ali Larijani](/people/ali-larijani), former president [Mahmoud Ahmadinejad](/people/mahmoud-ahmadinejad), and First Vice President Eshaq Jahangiri. [Saeed Jalili](/people/saeed-jalili) withdrew before election day to consolidate the hardliner vote. [Ebrahim Raisi](/people/ebrahim-raisi) won with approximately 62% of votes cast. The disqualification of [Ali Larijani](/people/ali-larijani) prompted his brother [Sadegh Amoli Larijani](/people/sadegh-larijani) to resign from the [Guardian Council](/orgs/guardian-council) in protest.
editorial_notes: Record-low turnout. 585 of 592 candidates disqualified. Raisi's win was the culmination of the hardliner consolidation of power that began with the 2020 parliamentary sweep.
